message: Clients are not required to present a certificate for app 'ref3tier-dev-app-7mog1c' in subscription 'Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7)'.
status_detail: Clients are not required to present a certificate for app 'ref3tier-dev-app-7mog1c' in subscription 'Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7)'.

unmapped.notes: Utilizing and maintaining client certificates will require additional work to obtain and manage replacement and key rotation.

finding_info.desc: **Azure App Service apps** enforce **mutual TLS** when `client certificate mode` is set to `Required`, meaning every inbound request must present a valid client certificate that the app can validate.
finding_info.title: Web app requires incoming client certificates

remediation.desc:
Set `client certificate mode` to `Required` and validate client certs in application logic (issuer, validity, revocation).

Enforce HTTPS only, avoid broad exclusion paths, and manage certs via a trusted CA with rotation and revocation. Apply **least privilege** and **zero trust**, layering with private access or IP restrictions *as needed*.

risk_details: Without **mTLS**, clients aren't cryptographically authenticated at the transport layer. Adversaries can reach endpoints using spoofed sources or stolen tokens, leading to unauthorized data access (confidentiality), request tampering (integrity), and automated abuse that degrades service (availability).

message: Authentication is not set up for app 'ref3tier-dev-app-7mog1c' in subscription 'Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7)'.
status_detail: Authentication is not set up for app 'ref3tier-dev-app-7mog1c' in subscription 'Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7)'.

unmapped.notes: This is only required for App Services which require authentication. Enabling on site like a marketing or support website will prevent unauthenticated access which would be undesirable. Adding Authentication requirement will increase cost of App Service and require additional security components to facilitate the authentication

finding_info.desc:
**Azure App Service** can enforce built-in **Authentication/Authorization** (Easy Auth) so requests are authenticated by a provider before reaching app code.

This evaluates whether platform auth is enabled for the app and an identity provider is configured.
finding_info.title: App Service app has App Service Authentication enabled

remediation.desc: Enable App Service **Authentication/Authorization** and set `Require authentication` for unauthenticated requests. Use **Microsoft Entra** or a trusted IdP, restrict tenants/audiences, enforce HTTPS, and apply **least privilege** with role/claim checks and Conditional Access for defense-in-depth. Avoid `Allow anonymous requests`.

risk_details: Without platform **authentication**, apps may accept **anonymous requests**, enabling unauthorized access to APIs and data. Attackers can enumerate endpoints and bypass weak app checks, risking data exposure (C), unauthorized changes (I), and automated abuse impacting availability (A).

message: HTTP is redirected to HTTPS for app 'ref3tier-dev-app-7mog1c' in subscription 'Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7)'.
status_detail: HTTP is redirected to HTTPS for app 'ref3tier-dev-app-7mog1c' in subscription 'Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7)'.

unmapped.notes: When it is enabled, every incoming HTTP request is redirected to the HTTPS port. This means an extra level of security will be added to the HTTP requests made to the app.

finding_info.desc: **Azure App Service web apps** redirect `HTTP` traffic to `HTTPS` when the `HTTPS Only` setting is enabled. This evaluation identifies apps that do not force secure transport by checking whether plaintext requests are automatically redirected to encrypted endpoints.
finding_info.title: App Service web app redirects HTTP to HTTPS

remediation.desc:
Enforce **HTTPS-only** for all apps.
- Use trusted certificates and require `TLS 1.2` or later
- Enable **HSTS** to prevent downgrade/mixed-content
- Redirect legacy `http` links to `https`
- Minimize HTTP exposure via WAF/CDN or private access
Apply **defense in depth** to protect data in transit.

risk_details: Leaving **HTTP accessible** enables **man-in-the-middle** interception, credential and cookie theft, and response tampering. This undermines **confidentiality** and **integrity**, and can lead to session hijacking or downgrade attacks that bypass TLS.

finding_info.desc: **Azure App Service web apps** are evaluated for **HTTP/2 support** via the `http20_enabled` configuration, indicating whether the site serves traffic using the HTTP/2 protocol
finding_info.title: App Service web app has HTTP/2.0 enabled

remediation.desc:
Enable **HTTP/2** (`http20_enabled=true`) to use a modern, efficient transport.

- Enforce `HTTPS Only` and a strong minimum `TLS` version for defense-in-depth
- Validate app/library compatibility before rollout
- Monitor performance and errors post-change; deploy gradually

risk_details: Without **HTTP/2**, apps remain on **HTTP/1.1**, increasing connection overhead and head-of-line blocking, which can reduce **availability** under load. Inefficient use of TLS sessions raises **DoS susceptibility** and degrades user experience, impacting service reliability

message: FTP is disabled for app 'ref3tier-dev-app-7mog1c' in subscription 'Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7)'.
status_detail: FTP is disabled for app 'ref3tier-dev-app-7mog1c' in subscription 'Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7)'.

unmapped.notes: Any deployment workflows that rely on FTP or FTPs rather than the WebDeploy or HTTPs endpoints may be affected.

finding_info.desc: **Azure App Service web apps** are evaluated for **FTP exposure** via the `ftpsState` setting. Values `FtpsOnly` or `Disabled` indicate FTP is not allowed; `AllAllowed` means both FTP and FTPS are accepted.
finding_info.title: App Service web app has FTP disabled or FTPS-only enforced

remediation.desc:
Disable FTP or enforce **FTPS** (`ftpsState: FtpsOnly` or `Disabled`).

Prefer **CI/CD** over manual FTP and apply **least privilege** with app-scoped credentials. Rotate publishing secrets, enforce modern TLS, and restrict access via private networking. *If FTP is unavoidable*, require FTPS and monitor publishing logs.

risk_details:
Allowing **FTP (unencrypted)** exposes credentials on public endpoints, enabling **credential theft** and **session hijacking**.

Compromise grants write access to code and content, enabling **malicious deployments**, backdoors, and data leakage, degrading **integrity** and **confidentiality**-with greater blast radius if shared, user-scope publishing credentials are used.

message: Minimum TLS version is set to 1.2 for app 'ref3tier-dev-app-7mog1c' in subscription 'Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7)'.
status_detail: Minimum TLS version is set to 1.2 for app 'ref3tier-dev-app-7mog1c' in subscription 'Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7)'.

unmapped.notes: By default, TLS Version feature will be set to 1.2 when a new app is created using the command-line tool or Azure Portal console.

finding_info.desc: **Azure App Service web apps** are assessed for the configured minimum TLS version for HTTPS. The expected baseline is `1.2` or `1.3`; settings that permit lower versions indicate acceptance of legacy TLS during client negotiation.
finding_info.title: App Service web app has minimum TLS version set to 1.2 or 1.3

remediation.desc: Enforce a minimum of `TLS 1.2` (prefer `1.3`) and disable `1.0/1.1`. Require **HTTPS-only**, enable HSTS, and align with modern cipher suites. Test client compatibility and phase out legacy agents. Document narrow exceptions with compensating controls to uphold **defense in depth** and prevent downgrades.

risk_details: Allowing `TLS 1.0/1.1` enables protocol downgrades and weak cipher negotiation, exposing HTTPS traffic to **MITM** interception, credential theft, and tampering. This undermines the **confidentiality** and **integrity** of sessions and data in transit, and can enable account takeover via stolen tokens.

finding_info.title: App Service web app has a managed identity configured

remediation.desc: Enable a **managed identity** and use it for all service-to-service access. Apply **least privilege** on target resources and eliminate secrets from code and app settings. Leaked credentials enable **unauthorized access** to SQL, Storage, or Key Vault, leading to **data exfiltration**, tampering, and lateral movement. Secret expiry or revocation can break connectivity, degrading **availability**.

message: Container image scan is disabled in subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7).
status_detail: Container image scan is disabled in subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7).

unmapped.notes: When using an Azure container registry, you might occasionally encounter problems. For example, you might not be able to pull a container image because of an issue with Docker in your local environment. Or, a network issue might prevent you from connecting to the registry.

finding_info.desc: **Azure subscriptions** have **container image vulnerability assessment** enabled for **Azure Container Registry** via Microsoft Defender for Cloud (`ContainerRegistriesVulnerabilityAssessments`). Images in registries are evaluated for known package vulnerabilities in their packages and dependencies.
finding_info.title: Subscription has container image vulnerability scanning enabled

remediation.desc:
Enable **Defender for Cloud** image assessment for registries and adopt **shift-left scanning**.
- Block deployment of images with high-severity findings
- Rebuild from patched base images regularly
- Enforce **least privilege** on registry access
- Use image signing and admission controls

risk_details: Without registry scanning, **known CVEs** in images can reach runtime, enabling **RCE**, privilege escalation, and lateral movement. This undermines data confidentiality and integrity and can reduce availability through cryptomining or service disruption.

Apply **least privilege** to IoT identities, enforce **network segmentation** and private access, and use **defense in depth** with continuous monitoring, alert tuning, and periodic coverage reviews.

risk_details:
Without **Defender for IoT**, device activity lacks telemetry and alerting, degrading CIA:
- Compromised devices join botnets and exfiltrate data
- Abused device identities alter cloud twins and commands
- Lateral movement from IoT networks to Azure workloads
This blind spot increases dwell time and blast radius.

message: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps is enabled for subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7).
status_detail: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps is enabled for subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7).

unmapped.notes: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps works with Standard pricing tier Subscription. Choosing the Standard pricing tier of Microsoft Defender for Cloud incurs an additional cost per resource.

finding_info.desc: **Subscription settings** contain the `MCAS` integration for **Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps**, and the setting is `enabled`.
finding_info.title: Defender for Cloud Apps is enabled

remediation.desc:
Enable and keep the `MCAS` integration consistent across subscriptions.
- Apply **least privilege** to integration roles and data access
- Use policy to enforce the setting and prevent drift
- Practice **defense in depth** by correlating SaaS and cloud signals
- Review licensing and validate alert coverage regularly

risk_details: Missing integration leaves **Defender for Cloud** blind to SaaS context, weakening correlation of control-plane activity with app usage. Attackers can hide data exfiltration via cloud apps, abuse OAuth grants, or mask unauthorized ARM changes-impacting confidentiality and integrity and slowing incident response.

message: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint integration is enabled for subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7).
status_detail: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint integration is enabled for subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7).

unmapped.notes: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint works with Standard pricing tier Subscription. Choosing the Standard pricing tier of Microsoft Defender for Cloud incurs an additional cost per resource.

finding_info.desc: **Azure subscription** integrates **Microsoft Defender for Endpoint** with **Defender for Cloud** via `WDATP`. The setting's presence and enabled state at the subscription scope are evaluated.
finding_info.title: Defender for Endpoint is enabled

remediation.desc:
Enable the **Defender for Endpoint** integration in **Defender for Cloud** at the subscription scope and ensure agents are deployed on supported machines.

- Apply **least privilege** to onboarding roles
- Centralize alerting and response
- Use **defense in depth** with hardening and network controls to reduce attack surface

risk_details: Without this integration, servers lack **EDR telemetry**, automated onboarding, and unified alerts, shrinking visibility. Hands-on-keyboard intrusions, ransomware, and credential theft can persist unnoticed, enabling data exfiltration (**confidentiality**), unauthorized changes (**integrity**), and outages (**availability**).

message: App 'prowler-audit-sp' has a password credential valid for 364 more days (beyond the 30-day rotation threshold).
status_detail: App 'prowler-audit-sp' has a password credential valid for 364 more days (beyond the 30-day rotation threshold).

unmapped.notes: This check evaluates both password secrets and certificate credentials. Each credential is reported individually. Apps with no credentials are skipped.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra ID** app registrations are evaluated for **credential validity**. Each app's password secrets and certificate credentials are checked for expiration. Credentials that are already expired, expiring within 30 days, or have no expiration date are flagged.
finding_info.title: App registration credentials are not expired or expiring soon

remediation.desc: Rotate app registration credentials before expiration. Set expiration to 6-12 months maximum. Prefer **managed identities** or **federated credentials** over secrets. Monitor credential expiry with Azure Monitor alerts or Microsoft Entra workbooks.

risk_details: Expired credentials cause **service outages** when apps can no longer authenticate. Credentials without expiration violate **least privilege** by persisting indefinitely. Long-lived or leaked secrets enable **unauthorized API access**, **data exfiltration**, and **lateral movement** via the app's permissions.

message: Strong authentication is not enforced for tenant vigneshrajaramasamygmail.onmicrosoft.com: the MFA registration campaign is not enabled.
status_detail: Strong authentication is not enforced for tenant vigneshrajaramasamygmail.onmicrosoft.com: the MFA registration campaign is not enabled.

unmapped.notes: This check reports a single finding per tenant and passes only when both the MFA registration campaign is enabled and at least one strong method is enabled. Microsoft recommends phishing-resistant methods (FIDO2, certificate-based) over app-based push notifications.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra ID** authentication methods policy is evaluated for **strong authentication enforcement**. The check verifies that at least one strong method (Microsoft Authenticator, FIDO2, or X.509 Certificate) is enabled and that the MFA registration campaign is active to prompt users to enroll.
finding_info.title: Strong authentication methods are enabled with registration enforcement

remediation.desc: Enable **Microsoft Authenticator** and **FIDO2 security keys** as authentication methods. Activate the **MFA registration campaign** to prompt users to register. Disable weak methods (SMS, voice) after migration. Use **Conditional Access** to require strong authentication for all users.

risk_details: Without strong authentication methods, the tenant relies on **passwords alone** or weak factors like SMS/voice. Password-only authentication enables **credential stuffing**, **phishing**, and **brute force** attacks. Without registration enforcement, users may never enroll in MFA even when it is available.

message: Conditional Access Policy does not require MFA for Microsoft Admin Portals.
status_detail: Conditional Access Policy does not require MFA for Microsoft Admin Portals.

unmapped.notes: Conditional Access policies require Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 licenses. Similarly, they may require additional overhead to maintain if users lose access to their MFA. Any users or groups which are granted an exception to this policy should be carefully tracked, be granted only minimal necessary privileges, and conditional access exceptions should be regularly reviewed or investigated.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra Conditional Access** is evaluated for a policy that requires **multifactor authentication** when accessing **Microsoft Admin Portals** (Microsoft 365 Admin Center, Microsoft Entra Admin Center, Microsoft Exchange Admin Center, etc.). The check confirms an enabled policy targets **All users**, includes the Microsoft Admin Portals app, and enforces an **MFA** grant control.
finding_info.title: Conditional Access policy requires MFA for Microsoft Admin Portals

remediation.desc: Create a **Conditional Access policy** that requires **MFA** for the **Microsoft Admin Portals** app targeting **All users**. Exclude only **break-glass** emergency accounts and non-interactive service principals. Test in **Report-only** mode before enforcing. Prefer **phishing-resistant** MFA methods (FIDO2, passkeys) and apply **least privilege** principles.

risk_details: Without **MFA** on admin portals, attackers with stolen credentials can access **Microsoft 365 Admin Center**, **Entra Admin Center**, or **Exchange Admin Center** to modify tenant settings, escalate privileges, and exfiltrate data. This directly impacts **confidentiality**, **integrity**, and **availability** of all services managed through those portals.

message: Conditional Access Policy does not require MFA for management API.
status_detail: Conditional Access Policy does not require MFA for management API.

unmapped.notes: Conditional Access policies require Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 licenses. Similarly, they may require additional overhead to maintain if users lose access to their MFA. Any users or groups which are granted an exception to this policy should be carefully tracked, be granted only minimal necessary privileges, and conditional access exceptions should be regularly reviewed or investigated.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra Conditional Access** is evaluated for a policy that requires **multifactor authentication** when accessing the **Windows Azure Service Management API** (Azure PowerShell, Azure CLI, Azure Resource Manager API, etc.). The check confirms an enabled policy targets **All users**, includes the Service Management API app, and enforces an **MFA** grant control.
finding_info.title: Multifactor Authentication is required for Windows Azure Service Management API

remediation.desc: 1. From the Azure Admin Portal dashboard, open Microsoft Entra ID. 2. Click Security in the Entra ID blade. 3. Click Conditional Access in the Security blade. 4. Click Policies in the Conditional Access blade. 5. Click + New policy. 6. Enter a name for the policy. 7. Click the blue text under Users. 8. Under Include, select All users. 9. Under Exclude, check Users and groups. 10. Select users or groups to be exempted from this policy (e.g. break-glass emergency accounts, and non-interactive service accounts) then click the Select button. 11. Click the blue text under Target Resources. 12. Under Include, click the Select apps radio button. 13. Click the blue text under Select. 14. Check the box next to Windows Azure Service Management APIs then click the Select button. 15. Click the blue text under Grant. 16. Under Grant access check the box for Require multifactor authentication then click the Select button. 17. Before creating, set Enable policy to Report-only. 18. Click Create. After testing the policy in report-only mode, update the Enable policy setting from Report-only to On.

risk_details: Without **MFA** on the Service Management API, attackers with stolen credentials can use **Azure CLI**, **PowerShell**, or the **Resource Manager API** to modify infrastructure, escalate privileges, and exfiltrate data. This directly impacts **confidentiality**, **integrity**, and **availability** of all Azure resources managed through the API.

message: There are 0 global administrators.
status_detail: There are 0 global administrators.

unmapped.notes: Implementing this recommendation may require changes in administrative workflows or the redistribution of roles and responsibilities. Adequate training and awareness should be provided to all Global Administrators.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra Global Administrator** assignments are evaluated by counting current role members per tenant and identifying when the number of assignees is `5` or more.
finding_info.title: Global Administrator role has fewer than 5 members

remediation.desc:
Limit the **Global Administrator** role to **fewer than 5** users.
- Apply **least privilege**; use narrower roles where possible
- Use **PIM** for just-in-time, no standing access
- Enforce **MFA** and dedicated admin accounts
- Run **access reviews** regularly and keep cloud-only `break-glass` accounts for emergencies

risk_details: Having **5+ Global Administrators** expands the privileged attack surface. Compromised credentials or tokens can enable tenant-wide changes, disable security controls, exfiltrate data, and create persistence, impacting **confidentiality**, **integrity**, and **availability** across Entra, Microsoft 365, and Azure.

message: Non-privileged users are able to create security groups via the Access Panel and the Azure administration portal.
status_detail: Non-privileged users are able to create security groups via the Access Panel and the Azure administration portal.

unmapped.notes: Enabling this setting could create a number of requests that would need to be managed by an administrator.

finding_info.desc:
**Microsoft Entra authorization policy** setting for default user role permissions governing creation of **security groups** by non-privileged users.

The value of `allowed_to_create_security_groups` is examined to ensure group creation is limited to administrators across portals, API, and PowerShell.
finding_info.title: Authorization policy disallows non-privileged users from creating security groups

metadata.name: Authorization Policy
metadata.description: Used to manage authorization related settings across the company.

remediation.desc: Restrict creation to **administrators** or a narrowly delegated role per **least privilege**. Set `allowed_to_create_security_groups` to `false` and use request/approval for new groups. Apply **governance**: naming standards, owner accountability, periodic **access reviews**, and monitor group lifecycle in audit logs.

risk_details: Allowing standard users to create security groups drives **entitlement sprawl** and can grant **unauthorized access** when those groups are tied to apps, sites, or roles. This weakens **least privilege**, complicates audits, and enables **lateral movement** or data exfiltration via misassigned group-based permissions.

message: App creation is not disabled for non-admin users.
status_detail: App creation is not disabled for non-admin users.

unmapped.notes: Enforcing this setting will create additional requests for approval that will need to be addressed by an administrator. If permissions are delegated, a user may approve a malevolent third party application, potentially giving it access to your data.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra authorization policy** controls whether default users can create application registrations via `allowed_to_create_apps`. App creation is expected to be limited to administrators or explicitly delegated roles.
finding_info.title: Tenant does not allow non-admin users to register applications

metadata.name: Authorization Policy
metadata.description: Used to manage authorization related settings across the company.

remediation.desc: Apply **least privilege**: restrict app registration to admins or delegated roles; set `Users can register applications` to `No`. Use the **Application Developer** role for exceptions, require **admin consent** workflows, routinely review app/service principal permissions, and audit changes for **defense in depth**.

risk_details: Permitting default users to register apps enables **unvetted service principals**, **consent phishing**, and **over-privileged API access**, threatening data **confidentiality** and **integrity**. Adversaries can persist with app credentials, exfiltrate mail/files, and perform **lateral movement** using rogue permissions.

message: Tenants creation is not disabled for non-admin users.
status_detail: Tenants creation is not disabled for non-admin users.

unmapped.notes: Enforcing this setting will ensure that only authorized users are able to create new tenants.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra authorization policy** governs whether default users can create new tenants. This evaluates if tenant creation is disabled for non-admin users via `allowed_to_create_tenants=false`.
finding_info.title: Authorization policy restricts non-admin users from creating tenants

metadata.name: Authorization Policy
metadata.description: Used to manage authorization related settings across the company.

remediation.desc: Apply **least privilege**: set `allowed_to_create_tenants=false` so only vetted admins or the **Tenant Creator** role (managed with **PIM**) can create tenants. Enforce **separation of duties**, require approvals, and monitor audits. Review this setting regularly to prevent tenant sprawl and maintain **defense in depth**.

risk_details: Permitting default users to create tenants fuels **shadow IT** and identity sprawl. Creators become **Global Administrators** of unmanaged tenants, eroding **confidentiality** and **integrity** through unsanctioned apps and unmonitored data flows, and degrading **availability** of centralized governance.

message: Guest invitations are not restricted to users with specific administrative roles only.
status_detail: Guest invitations are not restricted to users with specific administrative roles only.

unmapped.notes: With the option of Only users assigned to specific admin roles can invite guest users selected, users with specific admin roles will be in charge of sending invitations to the external users, requiring additional overhead by them to manage user accounts. This will mean coordinating with other departments as they are onboarding new users.

finding_info.desc:
**Microsoft Entra authorization policy** controls who can send **B2B guest invitations**.

Secure posture is when invitations are restricted to specific admin roles (`adminsAndGuestInviters`) or completely disabled (`none`).
finding_info.title: Tenant authorization policy restricts guest invitations to users with specific admin roles or disables guest invitations

metadata.name: Authorization Policy
metadata.description: Used to manage authorization related settings across the company.

remediation.desc: Restrict invitations to `Only users assigned to specific admin roles can invite guest users`, or disable them where not needed. Apply **least privilege** (use dedicated Guest Inviter role), enforce approvals, allowlist trusted domains, and run periodic access reviews with audit monitoring to remove stale or risky guests.

risk_details:
**Open guest invitation** rights let members or guests add external users without oversight, expanding the attack surface.

Impacts:
- **Confidentiality**: data leakage via overshared resources
- **Integrity**: privilege escalation through group/team access
- **Availability**: difficult containment due to account sprawl

message: Guest user access is not restricted to properties and memberships of their own directory objects
status_detail: Guest user access is not restricted to properties and memberships of their own directory objects

unmapped.notes: This may create additional requests for permissions to access resources that administrators will need to approve. According to https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise- users/users-restrict-guest-permissions#services-currently-not-supported Service without current support might have compatibility issues with the new guest restriction setting.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra authorization policy** guest settings are assessed to determine whether guest user access is limited to the properties and memberships of their own directory objects (`Restricted access`) instead of broader visibility into users and groups
finding_info.title: Authorization policy restricts guest user access to properties and memberships of their own directory objects

metadata.name: Authorization Policy
metadata.description: Used to manage authorization related settings across the company.

remediation.desc:
Apply **least privilege** to external users:
- Set guest access to `Restricted access` so guests can only view their own directory objects
- Avoid assigning admin roles to guests; use **PIM** for rare exceptions
- Constrain external collaboration and group visibility, and run periodic **access reviews** to remove stale guest access

risk_details: Excess guest visibility enables **directory reconnaissance**, exposing user and group details for **phishing**, **password spraying**, and targeted attacks. This weakens **confidentiality** and can facilitate **privilege escalation** and lateral movement through informed abuse of group memberships and access paths.

message: Entra allows users to consent apps accessing company data on their behalf
status_detail: Entra allows users to consent apps accessing company data on their behalf

unmapped.notes: Enforcing this setting may create additional requests that administrators need to review.

finding_info.desc: Microsoft Entra authorization settings are evaluated to determine if the default user role permits **user consent to applications**. The check looks at permission grant policies to see whether end users can authorize apps to access organization data on their behalf, or if consent is restricted (e.g., `Do not allow user consent`).
finding_info.title: Entra authorization policy disallows user consent for applications

metadata.name: Authorization Policy
metadata.description: Used to manage authorization related settings across the company.

remediation.desc: Enforce **least privilege** by setting user consent to `Do not allow user consent`. Use the **admin consent workflow** to review requests and pre-approve only vetted apps. *If needed*, allow consent only for verified publishers with low-impact scopes. Regularly review existing grants and monitor audit/sign-in logs.

risk_details: Permitting end-user consent enables **consent phishing** and over-privileged OAuth grants. Attackers can obtain tokens to read/send mail, access files, or act as the user, causing **data exfiltration**, persistence beyond password resets/MFA changes, and abuse of connected apps, impacting confidentiality and integrity.

message: Entra does not allow users to consent non-verified apps accessing company data on their behalf.
status_detail: Entra does not allow users to consent non-verified apps accessing company data on their behalf.

unmapped.notes: Enforcing this setting may create additional requests that administrators need to review.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra** authorization policy for the default user role is assessed for assignment of the user-consent policy `microsoft-user-default-legacy`. Its presence means users can self-consent to app permissions; its absence indicates consent is restricted (e.g., only verified publishers or low-impact scopes).
finding_info.title: Entra tenant does not allow users to consent to non-verified applications

metadata.name: Authorization Policy
metadata.description: Used to manage authorization related settings across the company.

remediation.desc:
Enforce **least privilege** for app consent:
- Remove `microsoft-user-default-legacy`
- Allow consent only for verified publishers and low-impact permissions (e.g., `microsoft-user-default-low`)
- Require admin approval for higher-risk scopes via the admin consent workflow
- Periodically review and revoke unused consent grants

risk_details: Broad self-consent enables **OAuth consent phishing** and rogue apps to gain tokens to tenant data (**confidentiality**), request write scopes to change resources (**integrity**), and persist via refresh tokens after password changes. Mis-scoped grants can drive lateral movement and privilege escalation.

message: Entra security defaults is enabled.
status_detail: Entra security defaults is enabled.

unmapped.notes: This recommendation should be implemented initially and then may be overridden by other service/product specific CIS Benchmarks. Administrators should also be aware that certain configurations in Microsoft Entra ID may impact other Microsoft services such as Microsoft 365.

finding_info.desc:
Microsoft Entra **Security defaults** provide tenant-wide baseline identity protections:
- MFA registration and challenges
- Legacy auth (`IMAP/POP/SMTP`) blocked
- Extra checks for privileged access

This evaluation identifies whether that baseline is enabled at the tenant level.
finding_info.title: Microsoft Entra ID tenant has Security Defaults enabled

remediation.desc:
Activate **Security defaults** or implement equivalent **Conditional Access** as defense in depth:
- Require MFA for all identities
- Block legacy authentication
- Safeguard admin portals and APIs
Apply **least privilege** and **zero trust**, and regularly review access patterns and break-glass exceptions to keep coverage complete.

risk_details: Absent these defaults, users can sign in with **password-only** or via **legacy protocols** that bypass MFA, enabling **password spray**, replay, and phishing-based takeovers. Compromise risks data exposure (confidentiality), unauthorized changes (integrity), and service disruption (availability).

message: There is no trusted location with IP ranges defined.
status_detail: There is no trusted location with IP ranges defined.

unmapped.notes: When configuring Named locations, the organization can create locations using Geographical location data or by defining source IP addresses or ranges. Configuring Named locations using a Country location does not provide the organization the ability to mark those locations as trusted, and any Conditional Access policy relying on those Countries location setting will not be able to use the All trusted locations setting within the Conditional Access policy. They instead will have to rely on the Select locations setting. This may add additional resource requirements when configuring, and will require thorough organizational testing. In general, Conditional Access policies may completely prevent users from authenticating to Microsoft Entra ID, and thorough testing is recommended. To avoid complete lockout, a 'Break Glass' account with full Global Administrator rights is recommended in the event all other administrators are locked out of authenticating to Microsoft Entra ID. This 'Break Glass' account should be excluded from Conditional Access Policies and should be configured with the longest pass phrase feasible. This account should only be used in the event of an emergency and complete administrator lockout.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access** supports **trusted named locations** defined by **public IP ranges**. Presence of at least one location marked `trusted` with IP CIDR ranges available for use in policy conditions.
finding_info.title: Entra tenant has a trusted named location with IP ranges defined

remediation.desc: Define **named locations** for your organization's egress IP ranges and mark them as `trusted`. Keep ranges accurate and narrow; review regularly. Use them in **Conditional Access** to enforce stronger controls off trusted networks. Apply **zero trust** and **least privilege**, and require MFA or device compliance when outside trusted locations.

risk_details: Without trusted IP-based locations, policies can't reliably distinguish corporate networks from unknown sources. This weakens **confidentiality and integrity**, enabling risky sign-ins to avoid stricter controls and forcing coarse rules that over-prompt users or leave **account takeover** and **data exfiltration** paths open.

message: Users can create Microsoft 365 groups.
status_detail: Users can create Microsoft 365 groups.

unmapped.notes: Enabling this setting could create a number of requests that would need to be managed by an administrator.

finding_info.desc: **Microsoft Entra** directory setting **Group.Unified** governs who can create **Microsoft 365 Groups**. The evaluation inspects `EnableGroupCreation` and, when present, `GroupCreationAllowedGroupId` to determine if group creation is broadly allowed or restricted to a designated group.
finding_info.title: Microsoft 365 group creation by users is disabled

remediation.desc: Apply **least privilege**: set `EnableGroupCreation=false` and allow only a controlled group via `GroupCreationAllowedGroupId`. Use **governed provisioning** with naming policies, sensitivity labels, and expiration/owner reviews. Monitor creation events and enforce **separation of duties** with approvals and lifecycle management.

risk_details: Unrestricted group creation drives sprawl of Teams, SharePoint sites, and mailboxes, undermining **confidentiality** via public spaces and guest invites. Compromised accounts can create groups to stage exfiltration or impersonation. It also heightens **integrity** risks from unsanctioned owners and **operational** burden for lifecycle and governance.

message: Role assignment ea275181-68fb-49c1-8caf-65a31c9c1b8f in subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not grant User Access Administrator role.

finding_info.title: Role assignment does not grant the User Access Administrator role

remediation.desc:
Enforce **least privilege**:
- Avoid standing `User Access Administrator`; use time-bound, approval-based elevation (PIM)
- Scope access to only required subscriptions/resource groups
- Require MFA and monitor role activity
- Review regularly and remove unused grants

risk_details: Persistent `User Access Administrator` enables assigning high-privilege roles and reading control-plane data, enabling privilege escalation and unauthorized access. Impact: **confidentiality** (data exposure), **integrity** (unauthorized changes), **availability** (service disruption).

message: Key Vault ref3tierkv7mog1c in subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have a diagnostic setting with audit logging.
status_detail: Key Vault ref3tierkv7mog1c in subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have a diagnostic setting with audit logging.

unmapped.notes: By default, Diagnostic AuditEvent logging is not enabled for Key Vault instances.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Key Vault** diagnostic settings capture **audit logs** (`AuditEvent`) when category groups `audit` and `allLogs` are enabled and routed to a supported destination. Logged events include management and data-plane operations on vaults, keys, secrets, and certificates.
finding_info.title: Key Vault has at least one diagnostic setting with audit logging enabled

remediation.desc: Enable **diagnostic settings** to collect `AuditEvent` logs-covering category groups `audit` and `allLogs`-and send them to a central sink. Apply **least privilege** to log access, enforce secure **retention/immutability**, monitor with alerts for anomalous operations, and use **separation of duties** to prevent logging bypass.

risk_details:
Without **Key Vault audit logging**, access and changes to keys, secrets, and certificates are untracked.

Attackers can misuse keys to decrypt data, alter or delete crypto material, and evade detection-eroding **confidentiality** and **integrity** and delaying **incident response**.

message: Keyvault ref3tierkv7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) is not using private endpoints.
status_detail: Keyvault ref3tierkv7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) is not using private endpoints.

unmapped.notes: Incorrect or poorly-timed changing of network configuration could result in service interruption. There are also additional costs tiers for running a private endpoint perpetabyte or more of networking traffic.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Key Vault** has **private endpoint connections** to serve secret and key operations over a private IP within your virtual network via Azure Private Link.
finding_info.title: Key Vault uses private endpoints

remediation.desc: Enable **Private Endpoints** for each Key Vault and disable public network access. Use private DNS so the vault FQDN resolves to the private IP. Apply **least privilege** with RBAC and managed identities, restrict traffic with NSGs and routing, and monitor access logs as part of **defense in depth**.

risk_details: Without **private endpoints**, the vault relies on a public endpoint, expanding exposure to scanning and misconfigured allowlists. Compromise can cause data theft (**confidentiality**), tampering or malware deployment (**integrity**), VM lockout or disruption (**availability**), and **lateral movement** within the VNet.

message: Security Group ref3tier-dev-data-nsg from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has SSH internet access restricted.
status_detail: Security Group ref3tier-dev-data-nsg from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has SSH internet access restricted.

finding_info.desc:
**Azure NSG** inbound rules that allow **SSH** on `TCP 22` from `0.0.0.0/0`, `Internet`, or `*` are identified, including rules where port ranges include `22` and protocol is `TCP` or `*`.

Indicates NSGs exposing SSH to the Internet.
finding_info.title: Network security group does not allow inbound SSH (TCP port 22) from the Internet

remediation.desc:
Apply **least privilege** on SSH:
- Remove public rules to `TCP 22` from `0.0.0.0/0`
- Allowlist specific admin IPs or management subnets
- Prefer **Azure Bastion**, **JIT access**, or **VPN/ExpressRoute** for admin
- Use key-based auth, disable passwords, and remove unnecessary public IPs

Adopt **defense in depth** with logging and periodic reviews.

risk_details: Public **SSH** access weakens **confidentiality** and **integrity**. Open `22` invites brute force and key theft, enabling remote shell control, persistence, and **lateral movement**. Attackers can pivot into VNets, exfiltrate data, deploy crypto-miners, and impact **availability**.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Virtual Network** subnets are evaluated for **Network Security Group (NSG)** association. Each subnet should have an NSG to enforce inbound and outbound traffic filtering rules. Subnets without NSGs allow all traffic by default.
finding_info.title: Subnet has a network security group associated

remediation.desc: Associate a **Network Security Group** with every subnet. Create NSG rules following **least privilege** — deny all by default and allow only required traffic. risk_details: Subnets without NSGs have **no network-level access control**. All inbound and outbound traffic is allowed by default, enabling **lateral movement**, **unauthorized access** to resources, and **data exfiltration** across the virtual network.

status_detail: Security Group ref3tier-dev-data-nsg from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has UDP internet access restricted.

finding_info.desc: **Azure NSG rules** are assessed for **inbound UDP** exposure to the public Internet (e.g., `0.0.0.0/0`, `*`, `Internet`). The finding identifies allow rules that permit unsolicited **UDP** traffic from any external source to attached resources.
finding_info.title: Network security group does not allow inbound UDP from the Internet

remediation.desc:
Apply **least privilege** on NSG rules:
- Deny Internet `UDP` inbound by default
- Allow only required sources/ports
- Prefer private access (VNets, private endpoints, VPN/ExpressRoute)
- Use **defense in depth** with Azure Firewall and DDoS Protection
- Monitor and disable or rate-limit unnecessary UDP services

risk_details: Publicly reachable **UDP** services enable **DDoS reflection/amplification**, exhausting bandwidth and compute and degrading **availability** for workloads and networks. Open services (DNS, NTP, SSDP, SNMP, CLDAP) can be abused with spoofed traffic, turning endpoints into amplifiers and disrupting adjacent resources.

status_detail: Policy assigment '/subscriptions/843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/SecurityCenterBuiltIn' from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) is configured with enforcement mode 'Default'.

finding_info.desc: **Defender for Cloud default policy assignment** (`SecurityCenterBuiltIn`) uses enforcement mode `Default` rather than `DoNotEnforce`
finding_info.title: Security Center built-in policy assignment has enforcement mode set to Default

remediation.desc: Keep enforcement mode `Default` on the default initiative and avoid disabling critical effects. Apply at scale for consistent governance, align with **least privilege** and **defense in depth**, validate changes in `Audit` in non-prod, and manage justified exceptions via time-bound policy exemptions instead of turning enforcement off.

risk_details: With `DoNotEnforce`, policy effects like `deny` and `deployIfNotExists` aren't applied, letting insecure configs persist. This erodes **confidentiality** and **integrity** (exposed endpoints, weak encryption) and can affect **availability** via unpatched or misconfigured services, enabling compromise and lateral movement.

message: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have any auditing policy configured.
status_detail: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have any auditing policy configured.

finding_info.desc: **Azure SQL Server** auditing is assessed at the server level to confirm audit logging is active. Configurations with any auditing policy state set to `Disabled` indicate auditing is not configured for the server and its databases.
finding_info.title: SQL Server has an auditing policy configured

remediation.desc: Enable server-level **auditing** and send logs to a centralized, tamper-resistant store with defined retention. Enforce **least privilege** and **separation of duties** for log access, integrate with monitoring for alerts, and periodically validate coverage. Use database-level auditing only for specific exceptions.

risk_details: Without **SQL auditing**, visibility into logins, privilege changes, and query activity is lost. Stealthy data exfiltration and tampering can go undetected, impacting **confidentiality** and **integrity**. Absent audit trails hinder **forensics**, slow incident response, and weaken compliance evidence.

message: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has auditing disabled.
status_detail: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has auditing disabled.

finding_info.desc: **Azure SQL Server auditing** settings are evaluated to ensure **auditing is enabled** and log retention is greater than `90` days. It considers the auditing policy state and the configured `retention_days` value.
finding_info.title: SQL server has auditing enabled with retention greater than 90 days

remediation.desc: Enable **server-level auditing** and set retention above `90` days, aligned with policy needs. Store logs in **tamper-resistant, centralized storage**, restrict access with **least privilege**, and integrate alerting and review. Apply **defense in depth** with continuous monitoring.

risk_details: Without adequate retention or with auditing disabled, **activity trails expire too soon**, limiting detection and investigation of **unauthorized access, data exfiltration, and privilege abuse**. This weakens **confidentiality** and **integrity** and slows incident response.

message: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have an Active Directory administrator.
status_detail: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have an Active Directory administrator.

finding_info.desc: **Azure SQL Server** is configured with a **Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) administrator** at the server scope, indicated by `administrator_type` set to `ActiveDirectory`.
finding_info.title: SQL Server has an Azure Active Directory administrator configured

remediation.desc: Assign a **Microsoft Entra administrator** (prefer a security group) at the server level and manage access via Entra groups. Enforce **least privilege**, require **MFA/conditional access**, and use **managed identities** for services. *If feasible*, adopt Entra-only authentication and phase out shared SQL logins.

risk_details: Without a **Microsoft Entra admin**, the server can't use Entra identities, pushing reliance on **SQL authentication**. This weakens confidentiality and integrity: no MFA/conditional access, harder offboarding and auditing, and compromised passwords can enable unauthorized data access and privilege escalation.

finding_info.desc: **Azure SQL Server** instances are evaluated for the server-level **security alert policy** of **Microsoft Defender for SQL**, expecting the policy state to be `Enabled`.
finding_info.title: SQL Server has Microsoft Defender for SQL enabled

remediation.desc: Enable **Microsoft Defender for SQL** across all servers and managed instances, preferably at subscription scope. Apply **least privilege**, restrict public exposure, and integrate alerts with your SOC. Regularly review **vulnerability assessment** results and harden findings as part of **defense in depth**.

risk_details: Without **Defender for SQL**, anomalous logins, SQL injection patterns, and risky configurations may go undetected, enabling data exfiltration (**confidentiality**), unauthorized changes (**integrity**), and disruptive queries or ransomware (**availability**).

message: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) is using version 1.2 as minimal accepted which is recommended.
status_detail: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) is using version 1.2 as minimal accepted which is recommended.

unmapped.notes: Verify support for the TLS version from the application side before changing the minimal version.

finding_info.desc: **Azure SQL logical servers** are assessed for the configured **minimum TLS version** for client connections. The finding determines whether the minimal accepted version aligns with recommended modern values such as `1.2` or `1.3`.
finding_info.title: SQL server enforces minimal TLS version 1.2 or 1.3

remediation.desc: Set the **minimum TLS** to `1.2` or higher (prefer `1.3` when supported). Upgrade client libraries and OS trust stores; remove legacy protocols and weak ciphers to prevent downgrades. Validate compatibility before enforcement and monitor connections for outdated TLS. Uphold **encryption in transit** and **defense in depth**.

risk_details: Without a modern minimum, clients can negotiate **weak TLS** or be downgraded, enabling **MITM** and decryption. This jeopardizes **confidentiality** (credential/data exposure) and **integrity** (query tampering), and may disrupt **availability** via session resets during handshake interference.

message: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has TDE disabled without CMK.
status_detail: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has TDE disabled without CMK.

unmapped.notes: Once TDE protector is encrypted with a Customer-managed key, it transfers entire responsibility of respective key management on to you, and hence you should be more careful about doing any operations on the particular key in order to keep data from corresponding SQL server and Databases hosted accessible. When deploying Customer Managed Keys, it is prudent to ensure that you also deploy an automated toolset for managing these keys (this should include discovery and key rotation), and Keys should be stored in an HSM or hardware backed keystore, such as Azure Key Vault. As far as toolsets go, check with your cryptographic key provider, as they may well provide one as an add-on to their service.

finding_info.desc: **Azure SQL Server** uses **Transparent Data Encryption** with a **customer-managed key** in Azure Key Vault, and each database has TDE `Enabled`
finding_info.title: SQL server uses a customer-managed key for the TDE protector and all databases have TDE enabled

remediation.desc:
Use a **customer-managed TDE protector** in Azure Key Vault or Managed HSM and ensure TDE is `Enabled` for every database.
- Apply **least privilege** to key access
- Enable **rotation** and monitor key use
- Protect keys with soft-delete and purge protection
- Enforce via **policy** and maintain key backups for restores

risk_details: Without **TDE with CMK**, data at rest may be unencrypted or controlled by service keys, weakening **confidentiality** and **key custody**. Attackers or insiders could read backups, snapshots, or stolen disks, and you cannot enforce **rotation**, **revocation**, or **separation of duties**, raising compliance and incident response risks.

message: Database ref3tier-dev-db from SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has TDE enabled
status_detail: Database ref3tier-dev-db from SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has TDE enabled

finding_info.desc:
**Azure SQL user databases** have **Transparent Data Encryption** (`TDE`) enabled, ensuring encryption of database files, backups, and transaction logs at rest.

*The `master` system database is excluded from evaluation.*
finding_info.title: SQL database has Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) enabled

remediation.desc: Enable **TDE** on all Azure SQL user databases. Prefer **customer-managed keys** in Key Vault or Managed HSM for control, rotation, and revocation. Apply **least privilege** and **separation of duties** to key access, enforce via **policy**, and monitor key/audit logs. *Maintain key backups and lifecycle to prevent availability loss.*

risk_details:
Without **TDE**, data at rest remains unencrypted:
- Stolen backups, snapshots, or compromised storage enable offline data disclosure
- Attackers with substrate access can bypass DB auth, harming **confidentiality** and enabling **exfiltration**

message: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have firewall rules allowing 0.0.0.0-255.255.255.255.
status_detail: SQL Server ref3tier-dev-sql-7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have firewall rules allowing 0.0.0.0-255.255.255.255.

finding_info.desc:
**Azure SQL Server** server-level firewall rules are evaluated for an entry that allows the entire IPv4 space (`0.0.0.0` to `255.255.255.255`).

The finding identifies presence of this Internet-wide rule on the server firewall.
finding_info.title: Azure SQL Server does not have firewall rules allowing 0.0.0.0-255.255.255.255

remediation.desc:
Remove the all-open rule and enforce **least privilege**.
- Restrict access to specific IPs/ranges
- Prefer **private endpoints** or VNet rules to avoid Internet exposure
- Layer controls (NSGs, Azure Firewall)
- Avoid broad exceptions like `Allow Azure services` and never use `0.0.0.0/0`

risk_details:
An Internet-wide rule permits unsolicited access from any host, enabling mass scanning, brute force, and exploitation of weak configurations.
- Confidentiality: unauthorized data access/exfiltration
- Integrity: malicious data/DDL changes
- Availability: resource abuse or DoS via excessive connections

finding_info.desc: **Azure Storage accounts** with **public network access** disabled cannot be reached from public networks. Setting `publicNetworkAccess` to `Disabled` overrides the public access settings of individual containers and forces access through private endpoints or trusted services. finding_info.title: Storage account has 'Public Network Access' disabled

remediation.desc: Disable **public network access** on the storage account and reach it through **private endpoints** or trusted Azure services only. risk_details:

This increases the risk of **unauthorized access**, **data exfiltration**, and reconnaissance against the account, especially when combined with weak network rules or overly permissive access policies.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Storage accounts** have **blob versioning** enabled (`IsVersioningEnabled`) to automatically retain previous versions of blobs created by updates or deletes
finding_info.title: Storage account has blob versioning enabled

remediation.desc: Enable **blob versioning** for accounts holding critical data. Pair with **blob soft delete** and lifecycle rules to retain and age off versions. Enforce **least privilege** on write and version-delete actions, and monitor access. *For high-churn data*, isolate into separate accounts with tailored retention to balance security and cost.

risk_details:
Without **blob versioning**:
- **Integrity**: overwrites can't be reverted
- **Availability**: deletes or ransomware remove usable copies
- **Forensics**: no immutable history for investigation and scoped recovery

Mistakes or compromised identities can cause irreversible object loss and wider impact.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Storage accounts** are assessed for whether **cross-tenant object replication** is disallowed via `AllowCrossTenantReplication=false`, limiting replication policies to the same tenant.
finding_info.title: Storage account has cross-tenant replication disabled

remediation.desc: Enforce `AllowCrossTenantReplication=false` and keep replication within the same tenant. Apply **least privilege** and **separation of duties** for replication management, backed by **policy-based governance** to prevent drift. *If cross-tenant transfer is required*, use formal data-sharing controls, monitoring, and time-bound approvals.

risk_details: Permitting cross-tenant replication can copy sensitive blobs into external tenants, undermining **confidentiality**. A compromised or mismanaged destination enables **data exfiltration**; mirrored updates/deletes can impact **integrity** and retention, complicating auditability and incident response.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Storage accounts** configure the **default network access rule** to `Deny`, so the **public endpoint** only accepts traffic from explicitly allowed virtual networks, IP ranges, or private endpoints
finding_info.title: Storage account default network access rule is set to Deny

remediation.desc: Set the default network access to `Deny` and permit only required sources: selected VNets, specific IP ranges, or preferably **private endpoints**. Apply **least privilege**, minimize service bypass, and use short-lived, scoped SAS to limit blast radius if credentials leak.

finding_info.title: Storage account uses Microsoft Entra authorization by default

remediation.desc:

message: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not encrypt with CMKs.
status_detail: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not encrypt with CMKs.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Storage accounts** use **customer-managed keys** (`CMK`) from **Key Vault/Managed HSM** for service-side encryption of data at rest, rather than platform-managed keys (`encryption_type`=`Microsoft.Keyvault`).
finding_info.title: Azure Storage account uses customer-managed keys (CMKs) for encryption

remediation.desc: Adopt **CMK** with keys in Key Vault or Managed HSM. Enforce **least privilege** for the storage identity, regular **key rotation**, and **separation of duties** between key custodians and operators. Audit key usage, enable tamper-resistant key protection (soft-delete/purge protection), and plan for **key revocation/crypto-shredding**.

risk_details:
Without **CMK**, keys are provider-controlled, reducing **confidentiality** and governance.
- Cannot promptly revoke access during incidents
- No custom rotation or separation of duties
- Limited key-use auditing
This weakens data sovereignty and hinders effective crypto-shredding.

status_detail: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have private endpoint connections.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Storage accounts** are evaluated for the presence of **Private Endpoint** connections. When configured, traffic flows over a VNet private IP via Private Link; when absent, access occurs through the storage account's public endpoint.
finding_info.title: Storage account has private endpoint connections

remediation.desc:
Prefer **Private Endpoints** for storage access and minimize public exposure:
- Limit or disable `Public network access`
- Use private DNS so names resolve to private IPs
- Enforce **least privilege** and **defense in depth** with segmentation and logging
- Monitor access and rotate keys/SAS *as part of routine hygiene*.

risk_details:
Relying on the **public endpoint** widens exposure:
- Confidentiality: higher risk of key/SAS compromise and unauthorized reads
- Integrity: abused creds enable writes/deletes
- Availability: subject to DDoS and internet scanning
It can also bypass egress controls, easing covert data exfiltration.

message: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has soft delete disabled.
status_detail: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has soft delete disabled.

unmapped.notes: Additional storage costs may be incurred as snapshots are retained.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Storage accounts** have **container soft delete** enabled via a retention policy that keeps deleted containers for a set period.
finding_info.title: Storage account has soft delete for containers enabled

remediation.desc: Enable **container soft delete** and choose a retention window (`7-365` days) that meets your RPO. Pair with **blob soft delete** and **versioning** for layered recovery. Enforce **least privilege** on delete actions and apply resource **locks** to prevent destructive changes.

risk_details: Without this, container deletions are permanent, reducing **availability** and **integrity**. A compromised user or faulty automation could erase entire datasets, forcing slow restores from backups and extending RTO/RPO, with potential downstream app outages.

message: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have Geo-redundant storage enabled, it has Standard_LRS instead.
status_detail: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have Geo-redundant storage enabled, it has Standard_LRS instead.

finding_info.desc:
**Azure Storage accounts** configured for **geo-redundant replication** via `Standard_GRS`, `Standard_GZRS`, `Standard_RAGRS`, or `Standard_RAGZRS`.

The setting indicates data is copied to a paired secondary region, with `RA-*` allowing read access during primary-region unavailability.
finding_info.title: Azure Storage account uses geo-redundant replication (GRS, GZRS, RA-GRS, or RA-GZRS)

remediation.desc: Adopt **GRS/GZRS** for critical workloads (prefer `Standard_GZRS` where supported) to achieve cross-region resilience. *If read continuity is required*, use `Standard_RAGRS` or `Standard_RAGZRS`. Define RPO/RTO, regularly test failover, and design for **defense in depth** across regions and zones.

risk_details: Absent **geo-replication**, data resides in one region, undermining **availability** and **durability** during regional failures. Disasters can cause prolonged downtime or unrecoverable loss. With geo-replication but without `RA-*`, the secondary is unreadable, increasing RTO and interrupting business continuity.

message: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has infrastructure encryption disabled.
status_detail: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has infrastructure encryption disabled.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Storage accounts** have **infrastructure encryption** enabled, providing **double encryption at rest** alongside service-level encryption (`requireInfrastructureEncryption=true`).
finding_info.title: Storage account has infrastructure encryption enabled

remediation.desc: Enable **infrastructure encryption** for accounts or scopes handling sensitive data to strengthen **defense in depth**. Plan it at creation, as the setting is immutable. Maintain strong key hygiene for service-level encryption (use CMK where appropriate, rotate, restrict access) and enforce guardrails with policy.

risk_details: Without this second layer, compromise of the service-level key or algorithm can expose stored data, degrading **confidentiality** and weakening **defense in depth**. Insider misuse or key theft is more likely to yield readable blobs, files, or tables.

message: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has no key expiration period set.
status_detail: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has no key expiration period set.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Storage accounts** must define a `key expiration period` for access-key rotation, with a maximum of `90` days. The evaluation looks for accounts lacking this setting or exceeding that limit.
finding_info.title: Storage account has access key expiration period set to 90 days or less

remediation.desc: Enforce a `key expiration period` of `<= 90` days and automate rotation. Prefer **Microsoft Entra ID** with managed identities over Shared Key; when SAS is needed, use user-delegation SAS. Apply **least privilege**, minimize key distribution, monitor usage, rotate on suspected exposure, and disable Shared Key when feasible.

risk_details: Long-lived storage access keys undermine **confidentiality** and **integrity**: a leaked or reused key grants full data access and can sign SAS tokens. Extended validity enables persistent unauthorized access, data exfiltration, and tampering, and complicates revocation and incident response.

message: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has secure transfer required enabled.
status_detail: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) has secure transfer required enabled.

finding_info.desc: **Azure Storage accounts** are evaluated for **secure transfer enforcement**, requiring all client requests to use `HTTPS` only (`enableHttpsTrafficOnly`) and blocking `HTTP`.
finding_info.title: Storage account has secure transfer required enabled

remediation.desc:
Enforce **HTTPS-only** on all storage accounts (`enableHttpsTrafficOnly`) and use modern TLS.
Apply **least privilege** to SAS and keys, rotate if exposure is suspected, and use **defense in depth**: prefer private endpoints, restrict public access, block `HTTP` at network controls, and ensure all clients use `https://` endpoints.

risk_details:
Allowing `HTTP` to storage endpoints enables **man-in-the-middle** and **TLS-stripping** attacks.
Intercepted traffic can expose credentials, SAS tokens, or data (**confidentiality**) and allow request tampering (**integrity**), leading to unauthorized access and **data exfiltration**.

message: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have SMB channel encryption enabled for file shares.
status_detail: Storage account ref3tierst7mog1c from subscription Azure subscription 1 (843e85de-3f5f-4614-967a-9f39f1fe9ba7) does not have SMB channel encryption enabled for file shares.

unmapped.notes: This check passes only if every SMB channel encryption algorithm allowed on the file shares is in the recommended list, which is configurable via azure.recommended_smb_channel_encryption_algorithms and defaults to AES-256-GCM only, as required by CIS.

finding_info.desc:
**Azure Storage file shares (SMB)** are evaluated for **SMB channel encryption** and whether the allowed ciphers include the recommended `AES-256-GCM`.

This identifies if encryption is configured and a secure algorithm is present in the SMB settings for file shares within the storage account.
finding_info.title: Storage account uses AES-256-GCM for SMB channel encryption on file shares

remediation.desc:
Enforce **defense in depth** by restricting SMB channel encryption to `AES-256-GCM` on SMB `3.1.1`, removing weaker options.

- Prefer private access (private endpoints/VPN)
- Require secure transfer and modern TLS
- Apply **least privilege** on shares
- Validate client support and monitor connections during rollout

risk_details: Missing or weak SMB channel encryption undermines **confidentiality** and **integrity**. On-path attackers could read sensitive files, capture hashes, or modify data in transit. Allowing legacy ciphers increases downgrade risks and can facilitate **lateral movement**, eroding trust boundaries across networks.
